items.text:
Subject: Pottery Studio Reservation – Confirmation for May 4

Hi Jenna,

I'm confirming your reservation for the ceramics studio in Carver Hall, Room 12, on Monday, May 4, from 1:00 PM to 5:00 PM. This will give you a full afternoon to finish glazing your final project before the May 9 critique.

A few reminders:
- Please bring your own apron and at least three bisque-fired pieces ready for glazing.
- The $25 kiln-firing fee must be paid in cash to the studio assistant before you begin.
- Studio rules require closed-toe shoes; you won't be admitted in sandals.

If you need to cancel, please email me at least 48 hours in advance so another student can use the slot. Looking forward to seeing your finished work!

Best,
Marcus Lee
Ceramics Lab Coordinator

format_metadata.subject: Pottery Studio Reservation – Confirmation for May 4

questions.stem: According to the email, what must Jenna bring to the studio on May 4?

options.A: A printed copy of the syllabus and her student ID
options.B: An apron and three bisque-fired ceramic pieces
options.C: A cash deposit of forty-five dollars in advance
options.D: A sketchbook and a list of glaze color preferences

questions.explanation: The email instructs Jenna to bring her own apron and at least three bisque-fired pieces ready for glazing.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the May 9 critique?

options.A: It will be held in a different building from the studio
options.B: It will replace the final exam for ceramics students
options.C: It is the deadline for showing completed ceramic work
options.D: It will include a public exhibition for visitors

questions.explanation: The email notes the May 4 booking will let Jenna finish glazing before the May 9 critique, implying the critique is when finished pieces must be presented.

questions.stem: What is the main purpose of this email?

options.A: To confirm a studio reservation and list requirements
options.B: To announce a new policy for the ceramics department this semester
options.C: To request that students vote on new studio operating hours
options.D: To advertise an upcoming ceramic art exhibition on campus

questions.explanation: The email opens by confirming Jenna's studio reservation and then lists what she must bring, pay, and wear, so its main purpose is to confirm the booking and outline requirements.
